name: County of Belalcázar Trail - Stage 2
description: Jog the easy 2.6-mile County of Belalcázar Trail - Stage 2, gaining 95 feet in about 27 minutes, past Castillo de Belalcázar.
region_intro: Jogging around Belalcázar offers routes through mountainous terrain and rolling hills, providing varied elevation changes. The region is characterized by lush, green landscapes and is dotted with extensive coffee plantations. From certain viewpoints, runners can enjoy panoramic views that include the Cauca River. This environment provides diverse ecosystems for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Belalcázar The most popular jogging route is Ermita del Calvario Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.4 km) trail that takes 40 minutes to complete. This moderate trail offers varied terrain and elevation changes. Another top favourite among local runners is County of Belalcázar Trail - Stage 2, an easy 2.6 miles (4.2 km) path. This easy path provides a gentle running experience through the local landscape. Local runners also love the Castle of Belalcázar loop from Belalcázar, a 15.1 miles (24.4 km) trail leading through the surrounding countryside and historical sites, often completed in about 4 hours 25 minutes. Jogging around Belalcázar is defined by mountainous terrain, rolling hills, and coffee plantations. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy runs to challenging long-distance trails. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 40 runners have used komoot to explore Belalcázar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Belalcázar?
A: Belalcázar offers a selection of 9 running routes, catering to various skill levels. These include easy paths, moderate trails, and more challenging long-distance options through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Belalcázar?
A: Yes, for those seeking a gentle running experience, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1001070475/county-of-belalcazar-trail-stage-2" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;County of Belalcázar Trail - Stage 2&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This easy 2.6-mile (4.2 km) path provides a pleasant run through the local landscape with minimal elevation changes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Belalcázar?
A: The running trails in Belalcázar are characterized by mountainous terrain and rolling hills, offering varied elevation changes. You'll jog through lush, green landscapes, often passing extensive coffee plantations, providing both scenic beauty and a cultural experience.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options for experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For a more challenging and longer run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belalcázar loop from Belalcázar&lt;/a&gt;. This route spans 15.1 miles (24.4 km) and leads through the surrounding countryside and historical sites, typically taking around 4 hours 25 minutes to complete.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or attractions I can see while running in Belalcázar?
A: From certain vantage points along the routes, such as near the monument to Cristo Rey, you can enjoy panoramic views that include the Cauca River and surrounding municipalities. Additionally, you might encounter historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5311931" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belalcázar Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of natural beauty and cultural interest.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer a circular experience. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31454888" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Belalcázar loop from Belalcázar&lt;/a&gt;, which allows you to start and end in the same location after exploring the countryside and historical sites.

Q: What is the most popular running trail in Belalcázar?
A: The most popular jogging route among local runners is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992977759/ermita-del-calvario-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Calvario Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate 3.4-mile (5.4 km) trail offers varied terrain and elevation changes, making it a favorite for many.

Q: Can I expect significant elevation changes on the running trails?
A: Yes, Belalcázar is nestled amidst the mountainous terrain of the central and western Andes, so many routes feature significant elevation changes. This provides a challenging and rewarding experience for runners looking to test their endurance, such as on sections like the Nápoles (Comehombres) Climb.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass through coffee plantations?
A: Absolutely. The region is dotted with extensive coffee plantations, and many routes will take you directly through these picturesque agricultural landscapes. Jogging through these areas offers a unique blend of scenic beauty and a glimpse into the local culture.

Q: What other towns or areas can I explore with running routes near Belalcázar?
A: While focusing on Belalcázar, you can also find running opportunities in nearby areas. For instance, there's a &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24523338" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hinojosa del Duque loop from Hinojosa del Duque&lt;/a&gt;, offering another moderate option for exploring the broader region.

Q: What kind of natural features define the running experience in Belalcázar?
A: The running experience in Belalcázar is defined by its verdant mountains, rolling hills, and diverse ecosystems. You'll encounter lush, green landscapes, extensive coffee plantations, and from higher points, breathtaking panoramic views, including the majestic Cauca River.
tips: Construction of the XV century also known as Castillo de Gahete because that was the previous name of the town in which it is located. But the beauty of its fortress ended up changing the name. The keep is amazing, disproportionate with its 47 m height, which makes it the tallest of its kind in the Iberian Peninsula. Despite its degree of protection, it is not in a very good state of conservation.

Sebastián de Belalcázar, founder of the colonial city of Quito, in Ecuador, was from this city. Its landscape is dominated by its imposing castle, but the old town houses other architectural jewels that are also worth visiting.

faqs.question: What is the estimated jogging time for this route?
faqs.answer: The County of Belalcázar Trail - Stage 2 is an easy jogging route, approximately 4.17 km long. Most joggers can expect to complete it in about 25-30 minutes, depending on their pace.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: The route passes by significant historical landmarks. You'll have the opportunity to see the impressive Castillo de Belalcázar and the historic Church of Santiago el Mayor of Belalcázar.

faqs.question: Is the County of Belalcázar Trail - Stage 2 suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this trail is rated as easy. With minimal elevation gain (around 29 meters) and a relatively short distance, it's a great option for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the general nature of an easy jogging trail, spring and autumn typically offer the most pleasant weather conditions for this route, with milder temperatures. Early mornings or late afternoons can also be ideal during warmer months.

faqs.question: Is the trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this localized route are not widely documented, it's generally good practice to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas near historical sites or if passing through any populated sections. Always ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: There is no widely documented information indicating that a permit or entrance fee is required to jog the County of Belalcázar Trail - Stage 2. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this localized trail is not readily available. However, as the route is associated with the town of Belalcázar and its landmarks, it's advisable to look for public parking options within the town, particularly near the Castillo de Belalcázar or the Church of Santiago el Mayor of Belalcázar, which are highlights along the route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the County of Belalcázar Trail - Stage 2?
faqs.answer: While specific terrain details are not extensively documented, as an easy jogging route, you can generally expect a mix of paved surfaces, well-maintained paths, or compacted dirt trails. It's suitable for standard running shoes.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route covers approximately 52% of the official trail known as Camino del Condado de Belalcázar - Etapa 2. This suggests it is part of a larger, named trail system.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water sources along the way?
faqs.answer: As a relatively short and localized trail, dedicated facilities directly on the route are unlikely. It's recommended to use facilities and carry sufficient water from the town of Belalcázar before starting your jog.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a jog on this trail?
faqs.answer: For this easy and relatively short jogging route, essential items include comfortable running shoes, appropriate athletic clothing for the weather, and a water bottle. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is also advisable, especially during sunny periods.
